etymology.description: 曲(曲) (qū, "bent; curved") + 辰(辰) (chén, "field") -> originally two hands holding a bundle of produce from the field -> farmer bringing goods to market
etymology.interpretation: The character originally depicted two hands holding a bundle of produce from the field, representing the act of farming and bringing agricultural goods to market. This image conveys the hard work and process involved in agriculture.
etymology.simplification: 农(農): The traditional character 農 was simplified by reducing the complexity and strokes of its components, creating the simpler form 农 with a similar overall structure.

examples.chinese: 农民正在田里工作(農民正在田裡工作)。
examples.english: The farmers are working in the field.

examples.chinese: 农业对国家非常重要(農業對國家非常重要)。
examples.english: Agriculture is very important to the country.

examples.chinese: 他决定回家乡从事农业(他決定回家鄉從事農業)。
examples.pinyin: Tā juédìng huí jiāxiāng cóngshì nóngyè.
examples.english: He decided to return to his hometown to pursue agriculture.
